woolf.name: Virginia Woolf
woolf.note: Locations are the Woolfs' main residence for each period; she also wrote many entries at Monk's House in the summers.

kafka.name: Franz Kafka
kafka.note: Kafka kept his notebooks in Prague, where he lived nearly all his life.

frank.name: Anne Frank
frank.source: The Diary of a Young Girl (Definitive Edition)
frank.note: From 6 July 1942 the family hid in the Secret Annex at Prinsengracht 263.

pepys.name: Samuel Pepys
pepys.source: The Diary of Samuel Pepys (Wheatley ed., via Project Gutenberg)
pepys.note: He wrote daily from 1660 to 1669, first in Axe Yard, Westminster, then beside the Navy Office in Seething Lane.

eno.name: Brian Eno
eno.note: Pinned to his west London studio, though 1995 took him all over the world.

warhol.name: Andy Warhol
warhol.source: The Andy Warhol Diaries (ed. Pat Hackett)
warhol.note: Dictated by phone each morning; travel days are pinned where he ended up.

hillesum.name: Etty Hillesum
hillesum.note: Written in her room on Gabriël Metsustraat, across from the Rijksmuseum, a few miles from Anne Frank's hiding place.

mukhina.name: Lena Mukhina
mukhina.source: The Diary of Lena Mukhina (tr. Amanda Love Darragh, 2014)
mukhina.note: A Leningrad schoolgirl's siege diary, kept at her aunt's flat at 26 Zagorodny Prospekt by the Five Corners; she was evacuated in June 1942 and survived the war.

luxun.name: 鲁迅
luxun.note: 教育部时期在北京，1926年南下厦门、广州，1927年秋定居上海。

jixianlin.name: 季羡林
jixianlin.note: 清华大学读书时期的日记，毫无顾忌，畅所欲言。

hushi.name: 胡适
hushi.note: 留美时期：康乃尔大学（绮色佳），1915年秋转入哥伦比亚大学（纽约）。

einstein.name: 爱因斯坦
einstein.source: 《爱因斯坦旅行日记》(泽夫·罗森克兰茨编，中译本)
einstein.note: 1922年10月—1923年3月的远东与中东之旅，途经锡兰、新加坡、香港、上海、日本、巴勒斯坦与西班牙；每篇日记都写于旅途中的不同地点。

darwin.name: Charles Darwin
darwin.source: A Naturalist's Voyage Round the World (the Beagle journal)
darwin.note: Kept aboard H.M.S. Beagle, 1832–36; each entry is pinned along the voyage's route, from Brazil and Patagonia round to the Galapagos, Tahiti, Australia and home.

bouton.name: Jim Bouton
bouton.note: A season with the Seattle Pilots, then the Houston Astros, spring through October 1969; each entry is pinned to the ballpark named in its dateline.

cook.name: James Cook
cook.source: Captain Cook's Journal During His First Voyage Round the World
